creator: alley1@llnl.gov
text:
Fujishima Satsuki wrote:
 
 >> Ed Alley wrote:
 
 >> I can do this with a send-pr each and every time a bug-fix
 >> is placed in the archive, but it will involve a patch that
 >> will consist of just a change to the version number like:
 >>
 >>	PORTVERSION= 1.5.02 ---> PORTVERSION= 1.5.03
 
 > Please do, with changes to distinfo.
 
 I see that this is the correct way now.
 
 >> Where can I find a definition of the meaning of "distinfo"?
 
 > ... However I believe it
 > is there to guarantee the distfile has not been trojaned.
 
 I think that this is THE key point that I missed! Dave Munro 
 (the creator of Yorick) and I have hashed this through and
 we believe that this is a good point.
 
 > There is a choise to use moving distfile for port. like this:
 
 > IGNOREFILES=	yorick-1.5.tgz
 > NO_PACKAGE=	"distfile is changing everyday"
 
 I am now convinced that this is not a good idea.
 
 Thanks for your patience with me. This is my first experience
 at the ports process. After my discussions with you and others,
 I see that there are well thought out reasons for how the ports
 are done.
 
 			Ed
